headline: The U.S. government forced Anthropic to pull its two most capable AI models worldwide, as criminals weaponized Google's Gemini for phishing and attackers ransacked Oracle ERP systems through a fresh zero-day.

emerging_trends.subject: AI Crossfire
emerging_trends.text: Governments and criminals pulled AI in opposite directions, with Washington yanking Anthropic's top models offline the same day a Chinese network bent Google's Gemini to mass phishing.

emerging_trends.subject: Supply Chain
emerging_trends.text: Package repositories and developer credentials drew attackers, from 400 hijacked Arch AUR packages to dark-web sales of GitHub access that seed software supply-chain footholds.

emerging_trends.subject: Long Dwell
emerging_trends.text: Aging footholds surfaced as Velvet Ant's near-decade inside Linux login code and a ten-year-old phpBB authentication bypass both showed how long intruders and flaws persist unseen.

emerging_trends.subject: Breach Penalties
emerging_trends.text: Regulators and courts attached growing price tags to breaches, with Coupang's record 409 million dollar fine and a 47 million dollar 23andMe settlement landing the same day.

topics.title: Anthropic Pulls Fable 5 and Mythos 5
topics.last_24h: Anthropic disabled Claude Fable 5 and Mythos 5 worldwide after a government order at 5:21 p.m. ET to bar foreign nationals from the models.
topics.summary: The Trump administration ordered Anthropic to block all foreign nationals, inside or outside the U.S., from its most advanced models, citing national security and export controls. Anthropic responded by suspending both models for every user worldwide. The company complies yet disputes the rationale, calling the cited jailbreak narrow and the underlying capability common across competing models. Dark Reading notes Mythos 5 upgrades the earlier preview, and Fable 5 is the version Anthropic hardened for general use.

sources.title: US Gov asks Anthropic to ban 'foreign national' access to Fable, Mythos
U.S. Orders Anthropic to Suspend Fable 5 and Mythos 5 Access for Foreign Nationals
Anthropic Says It Has Taken Its Latest AI Models Offline to Comply With New Export Controls
Claude Fable 5 Doesn't Change the Mythos Security Story

topics.title: Google Sues Gemini-Powered Smishing Network
topics.last_24h: Google filed suit against a Chinese cybercrime network it says weaponized its Gemini agent to mass-produce phishing texts aimed at Americans.
topics.summary: Google accuses the network of building and running a phishing-as-a-service kit called Outsider that leaned on Gemini to generate smishing messages targeting U.S. recipients. The suit escalates vendors' use of the courts against AI abuse. It also shows criminals folding mainstream AI agents into commodity fraud operations.

sources.title: Google Sues Chinese Smishing Network Accused of Using Gemini AI in Phishing

topics.title: Oracle PeopleSoft Zero-Day
topics.last_24h: ShinyHunters exploited an unpatched flaw in Oracle's PeopleSoft ERP to steal gigabytes of data from hundreds of organizations, hitting American universities hardest.
topics.summary: A critical zero-day in Oracle-owned PeopleSoft software lets attackers exfiltrate large volumes of data, and researchers rate it about as severe as bugs come. The extortion crew ShinyHunters seized on it, and higher-education institutions running the ERP suffered disproportionately. Organizations on PeopleSoft should hunt for signs of compromise and apply Oracle's fix the moment it ships.

sources.title: PeopleSoft 0-day affecting hundreds of organizations steals gigabytes of data
ShinyHunters Uses Oracle Zero-Day to Rampage Higher Ed

topics.title: Arch Linux AUR Hijack
topics.last_24h: Attackers took over more than 400 Arch User Repository packages and rewrote their build scripts to install a Rust infostealer and, with root, an eBPF rootkit.
topics.summary: Attackers compromised over 400 packages this week in the Arch User Repository, Arch Linux's community package collection. Each rewritten build script drops a Rust credential stealer that harvests developer secrets and access tokens on any machine that builds it, and when it gains root it loads an eBPF rootkit to hide. Developers who built AUR packages this week should rotate secrets and audit affected systems.

sources.title: Over 400 Arch Linux AUR Packages Hijacked to Deploy Infostealer and eBPF Rootkit
Over 400 Arch Linux packages compromised to push rootkit, infostealer

topics.title: Velvet Ant Linux Login Backdoor
topics.last_24h: Sygnia revealed that the China-nexus group Velvet Ant backdoored Linux PAM and OpenSSH components to hide inside the login system for nearly a decade.
topics.summary: The China-linked Velvet Ant group planted access deep inside the Linux authentication stack itself, the PAM and OpenSSH code that decides who may sign in. The placement let it survive routine cleanup and stay hidden close to ten years on a targeted network. Defenders should verify the integrity of PAM modules and SSH binaries on their most critical hosts.

sources.title: China-Linked Hackers Backdoored Linux Login Software to Hide for Nearly a Decade

topics.title: Record Breach Penalties and Payouts
topics.last_24h: South Korea fined Coupang 409 million dollars over a data breach, its largest ever, while a bankruptcy administrator approved a 47 million dollar settlement for 23andMe breach victims.
topics.summary: South Korea's privacy commission levied a record 409 million dollar penalty on the e-commerce firm Coupang, eclipsing the 88.8 million dollar SK Telecom fine from earlier this year. A U.S. bankruptcy administrator separately approved a 47 million dollar fund for roughly 7 million 23andMe customers whose genetic data hackers stole starting in April 2023 and posted on the dark web. Together the actions mark mounting financial consequences for failing to protect personal data.

sources.title: South Korea hits Coupang with record $409 million fine over data breach
Bankruptcy admin approves settlement fund of $47 million for 23andMe data breach victims
In Other News: Google Security Layoffs, AudiA6 Takedown, $400 Million Coupang Fine

topics.title: Section 702 Surveillance Lapse
topics.last_24h: Section 702 of the Foreign Intelligence Surveillance Act is poised to lapse after a legislative deadlock, the first such lapse since its 2008 passage.
topics.summary: Congressional deadlock pushed the foreign-intelligence surveillance authority known as Section 702 toward expiration for the first time since 2008. Intelligence agencies rely on the program to collect communications of foreign targets abroad, a capability that reaches domestic providers and sweeps in Americans' incidental data. A lapse leaves that collection in legal limbo until lawmakers act.

sources.title: Major US surveillance program poised to lapse after legislative deadlock

business_politics.text: The United States and Iran reached a final agreed text to end the war that began February 28, Pakistani mediators said, with the accord set to reopen the Strait of Hormuz and restore Persian Gulf oil and gas shipments. Oil futures settled at multimonth lows and Treasury yields fell for the week on the news.

sources.title: Pakistan says US-Iran deal to be signed within 24 hours
Deal With Iran Is Close, but Accord Will Kickstart Toughest Phase of Talks

business_politics.text: SpaceX jumped nearly a fifth on its trading debut after raising $75 billion, the largest initial public offering on record, valuing the rocket and AI company at $2.1 trillion and making Elon Musk the world's first trillionaire.

sources.title: SpaceX, Now Worth $2.1 Trillion, Pulls Off Goldilocks Debut
SpaceX's surge on debut makes Musk world's first trillionaire

business_politics.text: US headline annual inflation reached 4.2% in the latest reading, the highest since April 2023, narrowing the Federal Reserve's room to ease as it weighs a wait-and-see stance against the risk of letting price growth run.

sources.title: Chart of the Week: US headline inflation matters to consumers

business.text: Anthropic pulled its newest AI models, Fable 5 and Mythos 5, offline to comply with a Trump administration directive barring their use by foreign nationals on national security grounds.

sources.title: Anthropic says it has taken its latest AI models offline to comply with new export controls

business.text: Ford is recalling more than 250,000 Focus cars whose engines can stall while driving, after earlier fixes under a prior recall were done incorrectly.

sources.title: Ford recalls more than 250,000 Focus models because engines can stall unexpectedly

business.text: President Trump called the 2026 World Cup the most successful in history, though sluggish ticket sales cloud the claim as the tournament opens.

sources.title: Trump called the 2026 World Cup history's 'most successful.' Problems cloud ticket sales

around_town.text: Drivers parked along Ward Street in Oakland returned to find their cars towed; residents say they got no proper notice, while officials say the signage had been approved.

sources.title: Oakland residents say cars were towed without proper notice, officials say signage was approved

around_town.text: McKeesport school directors fired the entire staff of the high school football program amid a long-running controversy over head coach Matt Miller, who has led the team since 2016.

sources.title: McKeesport school directors fire football coach's staff amid controversy

events.text: The Bantam Jeep Heritage Festival returns to Butler this weekend, celebrating the town as the Jeep's birthplace with a vehicle invasion.

sources.title: Bantam Jeep Heritage Festival marks Jeep's birthplace in Butler, Pa.

events.text: Pogopalooza, the world championships of pogo, runs in Pittsburgh and Wilkinsburg this weekend after a downtown high-jump competition opened it Friday.

sources.title: Pogopalooza bounces back to Pittsburgh this weekend

events.text: Kings & Queens of the Court returns to the basketball courts on Barnes Street in New Kensington, drawing area kids for the next several Saturdays.

sources.title: New Kensington excited as Kings & Queens of the Court makes its return

reading.author: Ed Zitron
reading.title: Premium: The Silicon Valley Bubble (Part 1)
reading.summary: Zitron argues the AI era nears its end as OpenAI and Anthropic file to go public, racing for exit liquidity despite burning billions a year with no path to profitability.

reading.author: Stratechery
reading.title: Fable 5, Anthropic Alignment, AI Tiers
reading.summary: Thompson examines Fable 5 as the public version of Mythos, judging it highly capable while warning that its release sets troubling new precedents for alignment and tiered model access.

reading.author: Cal Newport
reading.title: Why Isn't AI Taking Our Jobs?
reading.summary: Newport challenges the AI-as-industrial-automation analogy, arguing that knowledge work resists wholesale replacement in ways the brawn-to-machine comparison overlooks.
